response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically capable polyglot staff engineer with over 15 years of individual contributor experience, but he is a poor match for a CTO role at an agentic AI fintech scale-up. His career contains no documented engineering leadership: no team building, no org scaling, no executive ownership, and no board-level communication. His AI and agentic systems exposure is extremely limited, confined to roughly one month at his current role with MCP tooling, far short of the demonstrated production multi-agent experience the role requires. Fintech exposure exists via a single senior developer role at RIDE Capital but carries no architectural or strategic depth in credit systems, payment flows, or regulated financial infrastructure.
strengths: Genuine distributed-systems production experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second, demonstrating real backend scale and low-latency engineering chops
Broad polyglot technical range across TypeScript, Node.js, C++, D, and embedded systems, signaling adaptability and low-level systems intuition
Consistent engineering-craft discipline evidenced by TDD advocacy, open-source library authorship, conference talks, and community mentorship across his career
concerns: No executive or people-leadership history whatsoever — no team scaling, hiring ownership, org design, or board communication; the entire career is individual contributor at staff or senior level

AI and agentic systems experience is superficial and brand-new, limited to one month of MCP tooling work with zero evidence of production multi-agent deployments, LLM evaluation pipelines, or AI systems in regulated environments
Fintech exposure is narrow and tactical, restricted to a single senior developer role at RIDE Capital with no evidence of owning credit infrastructure, payment flow architecture, or compliance design

key_factors.factor: Negligible production agentic AI track record
